visa_types.name: Business
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/prt/travel-insurance).
For the documents required checklist please click here.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fees applicable for Tourist, Family/Friend, Business,  Conference, Sports and Cultural are: -

[Table:Tourist]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in cash or debit, credit card in EGP. 
VFS service charge
Applicants (6 years and above) who apply at the Portugal Visa Application Centre who shall be levied a service charge of 2382 EGP inclusive of 14% VAT per application for payable in cash or credit.
Please  Note:
- The       applicable visa fee in Egyptian pounds is as per the current exchange       rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The       Visa fee & service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is       refused.
- Below are the additional optional services available at the visa application center at nominal price.
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, a VFS service charge in EGP 2123 (for submission with biometrics) inclusive of VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application Centre.(the VFS service charge is changeable based on the euro rate and can be changed without any notifications, and the difference amount can be paid or refunded on the same day of submission).

[Table:Tourist]
All optional services are non-refundable.
VISA Fee Exemption:
ALL TYPE OF VISA APPLICATIONS: 
- Children       under 6 years of age
- Scientific researchers, as regulated in recommendation No. 2194/761/EG of the European Parliament
- Representatives       of charitable organizations up to the age of 25 years, who travel to       partake in meetings or conferences
- Family       relatives of EU- (but not Portuguese) citizen, when travelling together or       upon relocation
visa_types.processing_time: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa varies accordingly to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application is given. The regular visa processing time is 15 working days upon completion of the application, the processing time may be extended to 30 or even 60 days calendar days, in cases that require a detailed assessment.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you plan to travel to Portugal for  Business, you must apply for Schengen visa (stay up to 90 days) or a national  visa (stay up to 6 months).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you plan to travel to Portugal for  Business, you must apply for Schengen visa (stay up to 90 days) or a national  visa (stay up to 6 months).

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for Tourist, Family/Friend, Business,  Conference, Sports and Cultural are: -

[Table:Tourist]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in cash or debit, credit card in EGP. 
VFS service charge
Applicants (6 years and above) who apply at the Portugal Visa Application Centre who shall be levied a service charge of 2382 EGP inclusive of 14% VAT per application for payable in cash or credit.
Please  Note:
- The       applicable visa fee in Egyptian pounds is as per the current exchange       rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The       Visa fee & service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is       refused.
- Below are the additional optional services available at the visa application center at nominal price.
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, a VFS service charge in EGP 2123 (for submission with biometrics) inclusive of VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application Centre.(the VFS service charge is changeable based on the euro rate and can be changed without any notifications, and the difference amount can be paid or refunded on the same day of submission).

[Table:Tourist]
All optional services are non-refundable.
VISA Fee Exemption:
ALL TYPE OF VISA APPLICATIONS: 
- Children       under 6 years of age
- Scientific researchers, as regulated in recommendation No. 2194/761/EG of the European Parliament
- Representatives       of charitable organizations up to the age of 25 years, who travel to       partake in meetings or conferences
- Family       relatives of EU- (but not Portuguese) citizen, when travelling together or       upon relocation

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/prt/travel-insurance).
For the documents required checklist please click here.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new  photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO  (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain       light-coloured background.
- The photo       must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted       picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of       the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–90% of the       photograph.
- Photo in       sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture       dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller       picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 237pixels by 320       pixels is not valid.
- On the other       hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within       limits. However, 450 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x       340 pixels one.
- Finally, the       image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image       resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to       control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better       printing quality.
- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The only       image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The       other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a       warning will be shown).
- Attaching        the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one       photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their  parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father,  one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the  father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph  presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered  incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa varies accordingly to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application is given. The regular visa processing time is 15 working days upon completion of the application, the processing time may be extended to 30 or even 60 days calendar days, in cases that require a detailed assessment.

## Download Forms
Please click here to fill the Visa form

visa_types.name: E- Visa ( Less than 1 year )
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/prt/travel-insurance).
General Requirements for all type of E-Visa
- Application form filled out and signed by the applicant, in case of minor children, form application form needs to be signed by both parents , to download the application please
click here
- One recent 35x45 mm photographs in color, of head on, with the uncovered head for gentlemen and white background.
- Passport :
- current passport must contain at least two blank pages
- The passport should be valid for at least 3 months after return from the  planned trip.
- photocopy of data page and previous Schengen visa
- Copy of all        non-empty pages has to be provided.
- Original Criminal record certificate translated and must be legalized from the embassy (children under 16 are exempted)
- Request for criminal record enquiry by the immigration and border services (SEF).
- Round flight ticket or Reservation       indicating the complete itinerary of the tour more than 90 days and less       than 365 days
- Proof of accommodation in Portugal.
- Rejection before of not (in case of       rejection before a copy of rejection letter should attached) + Copy of       National ID.
- Up-to-date original personal bank       account statement for the last 6 months and stamped.
- Valid travel insurance, allowing       medical coverage, including medical emergencies and repatriation and cover       the duration of stay. (Travel insurance from Portugal is accepted).
- Documents  to be provided by minors:
- Birth certificate translated.(copy + original )
- If the minor travel without his/her legal guardian: a power of attorney of both parents or legal guardian, the application needs to be signed by both parents in front of VFS Global agent, in case of one parent/guardian is residing in another country, he/she has to submit a written letter of consent to the travel.
- If the minor is travelling with one parent or one of his/her legal guardian : a power of attorney of the other parent, who isn’t traveling or a legal guardian needs to be submitted, in case if one parent/guardian is residing in another country, he/she has to submit a written letter of consent to the travel. Itinerary, if visiting more than one member state. Itinerary, if visiting more than one member state.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR MEDICAL TREATMENT.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR THE TRANSFER OF NATIONAL CITIZENS FROM STATE PARTIES TO THE WORLD TRADE ORGANIZATION, FOR SERVICE PROVIDING OR PROFESSIONAL TRAINING PURPOSES.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR SELF-EMPLOYMENT.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH, ACADEMIC TEACHING OR HIGHLY QUALIFIED ACTIVITY.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR AMATEUR SPORTS.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR STUDY PROGRAM AT AN EDUCATIONAL ESTABLISHMENT, STUDENT EXCHANGE PROGRAM, UNPAID PROFESSIONAL INTERNSHIP, VOLUNTARY WORK AND INTERNATIONAL COMMITMENTS RELATED TO THE FREE MOVEMENT OF SERVICES.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR ACCOMPANYING A FAMILY MEMBER UNDERGOING MEDICAL TREATMENT.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR SEASONAL WORK (UP TO 270 DAYS IN A 12 MONTH PERIOD).
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR ACADEMIC OR PROFESSIONAL TRAINING COURSE.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR ACCOMPANYING A FAMILY MEMBER HOLDING A TEMPORARY STAY VISA.
TEMPORARY STAY VISA FOR REMOTE WORK - “DIGITAL NOMADS”
For more information, kindly check the checklist. Click here

Overview
Application is applicable in case you are travelling for temp residency ( up to 1 year ). This visa is given for a travel period more than 90 days up to 365 days.

visa_types.name: D- Visa ( long Stay )
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/prt/travel-insurance).
General Requirements for all type of D-Visa
Important Note :
According to the latest instructions from the Portugal mission, All the citizens whose residence card has expired in Portugal and request to obtain a visa in order re enter Portugal, they are requested to apply for a long-term visa (including all required documents).
- Application form filled out and signed by the applicant, in case of minor children, form application form needs to be signed by both parents , to download the application please click here
- One recent 35x45 mm photographs in color, of head on, with the uncovered head for gentlemen and white background.
- Passport :
- current passport must contain at least two blank pages
- The passport should be valid for at least 3 months after return from the  planned trip.
- photocopy of data page and previous Schengen visa
- Copy of all        non-empty pages has to be provided.
- Original Criminal record certificate translated and must be legalized from embassy (children under 16 are exempted)
- Kindly note that applicants who wish to legalize their criminal record for visa purposes must send an email to the following address (sconsular.cairo@mne.pt (mailto:sconsular.cairo@mne.pt)) to obtain an appointment.
- Request for criminal record enquiry by the immigration and border services (SEF).
- Flight ticket one way
- Proof of accommodation in Portugal.
- Rejection before of not (in case of       rejection before a copy of rejection letter should attached) + Copy of       National ID.
- Up-to-date original personal bank account statement for the last 6 months and stamped or the husband/Wife bank statement from Portugal.
- Valid travel insurance, allowing medical coverage, including medical emergencies and repatriation and cover the duration of stay. (Travel insurance from Portugal is accepted).
- Documents  to be provided by minors:
- Birth certificate translated.(copy + original )
- If the minor travel without his/her legal guardian: a power of attorney of both parents or legal guardian, the application needs to be signed by both parents in front of VFS Global agent, in case of one parent/guardian is residing in another country, he/she has to submit a written letter of consent to the travel.
- If the minor is travelling with one parent or one of his/her legal guardian : a power of attorney of the other parent, who isn’t traveling or a legal guardian needs to be submitted, in case if one parent/guardian is residing in another country, he/she has to submit a written letter of consent to the travel. Itinerary, if visiting more than one member state. Itinerary, if visiting more than one member state.

For More Information ,Kindly find types of residency :
RESIDENCE VISA FOR EMPLOYMENT.
RESIDENCE VISA FOR SELF-EMPLOYMENT OR IMMIGRANT ENTREPRENEUR AND STARTUP VISA
RESIDENCE VISA FOR TEACHING, HIGHLY QUALIFIED AND CULTURAL ACTIVITY OR HIGHLY QUALIFIED ACTIVITIES CARRIED OUT BY SALARIED EMPLOYEES.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR RESEARCH, STUDY, STUDENTS EXCHANGE, INTERNSHIP AND VOLUNTARY WORK.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR FAMILY REUNIFICATION.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR ESTABLISHMENT OF RESIDENCE FOR PENSIONERS, RELIGIOUS PURPOSES OR PEOPLE LIVING ON THEIR OWN INCOME.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR ACCOMPANYING A FAMILY MEMBER HOLDING A RESIDENCY VISA.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR REMOTEWORK - “DIGITALNOMADS”.
For more information, kindly check the checklists.

Apart from the visa fee, a VFS service charge in EGP 2382 (for submission with biometrics) inclusive of 14% VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application Centre. (the VFS service charge is changeable based on the euro rate and can be changed without any notifications, and the difference amount can be paid or refunded on the same day of submission).

[Table:D- Visa ( long Stay )]
All optional services are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time: The legal processing time for a national visa is 30-90 calendar days.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Application is applicable in case you are travelling for long stay ( more than 1 year ). This visa is given for a travel period more than 365 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Application is applicable in case you are travelling for long stay ( more than 1 year ). This visa is given for a travel period more than 365 days.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for independent work purposes, Professional internship, Medical Treatment: -

[Table:D- Visa ( long Stay )]
The above-mentioned Visa Fee is to be paid in cash or debit, credit card in EGP.
VFS service charge
Applicants (6 years and above) applying at the Portugal Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 2382 EGP payable in cash or credit/Debit cards.

Please  Note:
- The       applicable visa fee in Egyptian pounds is as per the current exchange       rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The       Visa fee & service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is       refused.
- Below are the additional optional services available at the visa application center at nominal price.
Applicants applying at the Portugal Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 2380 EGP payable in cash or credit
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Egyptian pounds is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee & service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.
- Below are the additional optional services available at the visa application center at nominal price.
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, a VFS service charge in EGP 2382 (for submission with biometrics) inclusive of 14% VAT per application will be levied for applicants applying at the visa application Centre. (the VFS service charge is changeable based on the euro rate and can be changed without any notifications, and the difference amount can be paid or refunded on the same day of submission).

[Table:D- Visa ( long Stay )]
All optional services are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/prt/travel-insurance).
General Requirements for all type of D-Visa
Important Note :
According to the latest instructions from the Portugal mission, All the citizens whose residence card has expired in Portugal and request to obtain a visa in order re enter Portugal, they are requested to apply for a long-term visa (including all required documents).
- Application form filled out and signed by the applicant, in case of minor children, form application form needs to be signed by both parents , to download the application please click here
- One recent 35x45 mm photographs in color, of head on, with the uncovered head for gentlemen and white background.
- Passport :
- current passport must contain at least two blank pages
- The passport should be valid for at least 3 months after return from the  planned trip.
- photocopy of data page and previous Schengen visa
- Copy of all        non-empty pages has to be provided.
- Original Criminal record certificate translated and must be legalized from embassy (children under 16 are exempted)
- Kindly note that applicants who wish to legalize their criminal record for visa purposes must send an email to the following address (sconsular.cairo@mne.pt (mailto:sconsular.cairo@mne.pt)) to obtain an appointment.
- Request for criminal record enquiry by the immigration and border services (SEF).
- Flight ticket one way
- Proof of accommodation in Portugal.
- Rejection before of not (in case of       rejection before a copy of rejection letter should attached) + Copy of       National ID.
- Up-to-date original personal bank account statement for the last 6 months and stamped or the husband/Wife bank statement from Portugal.
- Valid travel insurance, allowing medical coverage, including medical emergencies and repatriation and cover the duration of stay. (Travel insurance from Portugal is accepted).
- Documents  to be provided by minors:
- Birth certificate translated.(copy + original )
- If the minor travel without his/her legal guardian: a power of attorney of both parents or legal guardian, the application needs to be signed by both parents in front of VFS Global agent, in case of one parent/guardian is residing in another country, he/she has to submit a written letter of consent to the travel.
- If the minor is travelling with one parent or one of his/her legal guardian : a power of attorney of the other parent, who isn’t traveling or a legal guardian needs to be submitted, in case if one parent/guardian is residing in another country, he/she has to submit a written letter of consent to the travel. Itinerary, if visiting more than one member state. Itinerary, if visiting more than one member state.

For More Information ,Kindly find types of residency :
RESIDENCE VISA FOR EMPLOYMENT.
RESIDENCE VISA FOR SELF-EMPLOYMENT OR IMMIGRANT ENTREPRENEUR AND STARTUP VISA
RESIDENCE VISA FOR TEACHING, HIGHLY QUALIFIED AND CULTURAL ACTIVITY OR HIGHLY QUALIFIED ACTIVITIES CARRIED OUT BY SALARIED EMPLOYEES.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR RESEARCH, STUDY, STUDENTS EXCHANGE, INTERNSHIP AND VOLUNTARY WORK.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR FAMILY REUNIFICATION.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR ESTABLISHMENT OF RESIDENCE FOR PENSIONERS, RELIGIOUS PURPOSES OR PEOPLE LIVING ON THEIR OWN INCOME.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR ACCOMPANYING A FAMILY MEMBER HOLDING A RESIDENCY VISA.
RESIDENCY VISA FOR REMOTEWORK - “DIGITALNOMADS”.
For more information, kindly check the checklists.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new  photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO  (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain       light-coloured background.
- The photo       must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted       picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of       the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–90% of the       photograph.
- Photo in       sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture       dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller       picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 237pixels by 320       pixels is not valid.
- On the other       hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within       limits. However, 450 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x       340 pixels one.
- Finally, the       image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image       resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to       control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better       printing quality.
- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The only       image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The       other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a       warning will be shown).
- Attaching        the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one       photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their  parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father,  one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the  father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph  presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered  incomplete.

## Processing Time
The legal processing time for a national visa is 30-90 calendar days.

## Download Forms
Please click here to fill the Visa form

## Documents Required

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new  photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO  (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain       light-coloured background.
- The photo       must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted       picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of       the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–90% of the       photograph.
- Photo in       sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture       dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller       picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 237pixels by 320       pixels is not valid.
- On the other       hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within       limits. However, 450 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x       340 pixels one.
- Finally, the       image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image       resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to       control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better       printing quality.
- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The only       image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The       other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a       warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their  parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father,  one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the  father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph  presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered  incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa varies accordingly to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application is given. The regular visa processing time is 15 working days upon completion of the application, the processing time may be extended to 30 or even 60 days calendar days, in cases that require a detailed assessment.
